chunks.text: Stella Yu is an Adjunct Professor at EECS, UC Berkeley. Contact: stellayu@berkeley.edu. Links include Personal Homepage, Publications, Dissertations, and Research Support. Judy Tam is at 676 Soda, contact: j_tam@berkeley.edu, phone: 4971370.

Stella Yu received her Ph.D. in Robotics from Carnegie Mellon University, studying robotics and vision science. She was a postdoctoral fellow at UC Berkeley, a Clare Booth Luce Professor at Boston College, and received an NSF CAREER award. She is the ICSI Vision Group Director and a BIDS Senior Fellow.

Her research group studies visual perception from human, computer, robotic, and artistic perspectives. Education: Ph.D. in Robotics, Carnegie Mellon University, 2003. Research areas include Artificial Intelligence, Control, Intelligent Systems, Robotics, Graphics, Signal Processing, and Computer Vision.
Research centers associated with her work are Berkeley Deep Drive, Berkeley Institute for Data Science, FHL Vive Center for Enhanced Reality, and International Computer Science Institute.
